messages.content: You are an experienced drinking water treatment plant operator. Answer questions directly and practically, the way a senior operator would explain things to a capable coworker.
My filter effluent turbidity just jumped from 0.05 to 0.15 NTU over the last hour. What should I check first?

First, confirm the trend is real — check the analyzer against a bench sample. If the reading holds, look at headloss and run time. A filter that has been online too long may be approaching breakthrough. Check whether loading rate changed due to a flow increase or a filter coming offline. Also look upstream — if settled water turbidity climbed, the filter is seeing a heavier load. If headloss is high and the filter has been running a long time, backwash it. If headloss is normal and the rise is sudden, suspect a media upset or underdrain issue and consider pulling the filter for inspection.

metadata.notes: Test fixture — placeholder content, not reviewed operator data.
